title: Radix Colors — Radix UI
insights: Gold standard for 12-step OKLCH color scales. Each accent has solid + transparent variants. Steps 1-2: backgrounds, 3-5: UI component backgrounds (normal/hover/active), 6-8: borders, 9-10: solid fills, 11-12: text (low/high contrast). APCA-based contrast targets

title: Scales — Radix Colors Docs
insights: Deep dive into the 12-step color scale architecture. Step 9 has highest chroma (purest step). Each step has defined semantic purpose. Auto-generates accessible dark mode by adjusting L and C

title: Use Cases — Radix Colors Docs
insights: Complete use-case guide for each step. Step 3=normal states, 4=hover, 5=selected, 6=subtle borders, 7=interactive borders, 8=focus rings, 9=solid backgrounds, 11=low-contrast text, 12=high-contrast text

title: Color — Radix Themes Docs
insights: How Radix Themes maps color tokens to components. Accent/gray/overlay/surface color roles. CSS variables mapping

title: radix-ui/colors GitHub
insights: Source for all Radix color scales as CSS/JS/TS. OKLCH values with P3 gamut support. Used as primitive layer by many design systems

title: OKLCH in CSS: Why We Moved from RGB and HSL — Evil Martians
insights: Definitive case for OKLCH migration. CSS Colors 4 spec uses OKLCH for gamut mapping. Perceptually uniform = equal numerical changes = equal perceived differences. Fixes blue/purple hue shifts in LCH. P3 gamut access. Token scale generation becomes formulaic

title: Better Dynamic Themes in Tailwind with OKLCH Color Magic — Evil Martians
insights: Combines OKLCH + CSS custom properties + Tailwind for dynamic themes. Adjust only L and C to switch light/dark/high-contrast without re-authoring palettes. Practical implementation pattern

title: Exploring the OKLCH Ecosystem and Its Tools — Evil Martians
insights: May 2025 OKLCH ecosystem map: atmos.style, Harmony palette, Harmonizer, Polychrom, apcach. Browser support 92%+ in Q2 2025. Essential tool survey

title: evilmartians/harmony GitHub
insights: OKLCH color palette using APCA contrast algorithm. CSS variables with RGB fallbacks. P3 gamut. Tailwind integration. MIT license. Previously unavailable P3 colors now accessible

title: Harmonizer — Evil Martians
insights: Web app + Figma plugin for generating accessible OKLCH palettes. Uses APCA. Consistent chroma across all hues and levels. Export to CSS variables

title: Atmos Style — OKLCH Color Palette Tool
insights: 30k+ designers use it. LCH/OKLCH-first UI palette builder. Builds accessible palettes with APCA (WCAG 3) and WCAG 2. Figma plugin. HEX/RGB/HSL/LCH/OKLCH converters. Export to design systems

title: Color — Material Design 3 (Material You)
insights: Dynamic color system. 5 key colors → 5 tonal palettes × 13 tones = 65 colors. Color roles: primary/secondary/tertiary each has 4 compatible tones (accent, on-color, container, on-container). Accessibility by default

title: Easing and Duration Token Specs — Material Design 3
insights: Official M3 motion token specifications. Easing curves and duration values as named tokens. Non-linear scale for perceived consistency across different animation distances

title: Design Tokens — Material Design 3
insights: Three-tier token architecture: reference (raw) → system (semantic) → component. Token concept central to Material You. Replaces hardcoded hex values with self-explanatory names

title: material-foundation/material-color-utilities GitHub
insights: Google's official color utilities for Material You. Implements dynamic color algorithm, tonal palette generation, color scoring. Multi-platform: JS, Dart, Swift, Kotlin

title: material-foundation/material-theme-builder GitHub
insights: Official Material theme builder tool. Visualize dynamic color, create custom Material themes from seed color. Reference implementation for M3 token system

title: Design Tokens — Adobe Spectrum
insights: Adobe's design decisions as data. Tokens integrated into component libraries, UI kits, Spectrum XD plugin. Covers color, typography, spacing, platform scales, color themes, component states. Migrating from spectrum-vars to Core Tokens ecosystem

title: adobe/spectrum-tokens GitHub (redirects to spectrum-design-data)
insights: Redirects to adobe/spectrum-design-data. Active development moved there. Beyond just tokens — comprehensive design data and tooling. npm: @adobe/spectrum-tokens

title: Spectrum Token Visualization Tool
insights: Interactive visualization of all Spectrum tokens. See values, references, usage across modes. Valuable for understanding token structure at scale

title: @adobe/spectrum-tokens — npm
insights: npm package for Adobe Spectrum tokens. Track version history and adoption

title: oklch() — MDN Web Docs
insights: Official MDN reference for oklch() CSS function. Cylindrical form of oklab(). L=lightness, C=chroma, H=hue + optional alpha. Full browser support as of 2024. W3C CSS Color Module Level 4

title: prefers-contrast — MDN Web Docs
insights: Media query for detecting user contrast preference. Values: no-preference, more, less, forced. Token systems must define high-contrast mode token set responding to this query

title: oklch() — CSS-Tricks Almanac
insights: Practical OKLCH reference with examples. Can be used anywhere CSS accepts color including color-mix(). Global support rate exceeded 92% in Q2 2025

title: OKLCH in CSS: Consistent, Accessible Color Palettes — LogRocket
insights: Practical OKLCH implementation guide. Consistent color scale generation using formula. Accessible token generation. Integration with design systems

title: Tailwind CSS v4.0 — Official Blog
insights: CSS-first configuration via @theme directive. All theme tokens are CSS variables. HSL → OKLCH migration. Zero JavaScript config for theming. @theme defines design tokens consumed by utilities AND available as CSS variables at runtime

title: Theme Variables — Tailwind CSS v4 Docs
insights: @theme directive: define design tokens (colors, spacing, fonts, radii, shadows, animations, breakpoints). Tokens generate utilities AND browser CSS variables. Single CSS-first source of truth. --color-*, --spacing-*, --font-* naming convention

title: Exploring Typesafe Design Tokens in Tailwind 4 — DEV Community
insights: TypeScript-safe token patterns with Tailwind v4 @theme. Token-only styling (no raw Tailwind literals). Type inference from CSS variables

title: Design Tokens That Scale in 2026 (Tailwind v4 + CSS Variables) — Mavik Labs
insights: @theme collapses design system sprawl into CSS-first source of truth. Define once → utilities generated → browser exposes as CSS variables. 2026 perspective

title: Naming Tokens in Design Systems — Nathan Curtis / EightShapes
insights: Canonical naming taxonomy reference. Levels: namespace-category-concept-property-variant/scale-state. Semantic tokens start with category. $esds-color-neutral-42 maps namespace+category+variant+scale. Foundational reading for all token teams

title: Reimagining a Token Taxonomy — Nathan Curtis / EightShapes
insights: 18 months redesigning token taxonomies for 4 different design systems. Process: identify problems → define criteria → implement across design+code+docs. Minimal but robust token count

title: Tokens in Design Systems: 10 Tips — Nathan Curtis / EightShapes
insights: Original canonical tokens reference from 2016. Recommends JSON as open standard for encoding hierarchical name/value pairs. 10 architecture tips still broadly applicable

title: Best Practices For Naming Design Tokens, Components and Variables — Smashing Magazine
insights: Intuit case study by Nate Baldwin. Language brings logic to color. Levels: category/concept/property/variant/state. Large multi-platform systems use 5-6 levels. Interactive naming playbook by Romina Kavcic

title: Creating a Flexible Design Token Taxonomy for Intuit's Design System — Nate Baldwin
insights: Intuit (QuickBooks, TurboTax, Mailchimp) flexible token taxonomy. Goal: intuitive + minimal set with broad use-case coverage. Tested with designers and developers from all consuming systems

title: Token Naming Reference — Fluent UI Token Pipeline
insights: Microsoft's comprehensive token naming reference. Global tokens = context-agnostic raw values. Alias tokens = semantic meaning. Two-layer Fluent token architecture

title: Token JSON Format Reference — Fluent UI Token Pipeline
insights: JSON format used by Fluent UI token pipeline (now transitioning to DTCG standard). Token file structure, sets, references. Deprecated in favor of standard DTCG

title: Design Tokens — Fluent 2 Design System
insights: Fluent 2 token architecture. Two-layer: global (raw hex) + alias (semantic). Stored values used to assign Fluent styles: color, typography, spacing, elevation. DTCG format transition underway

title: The Many Faces of Themeable Design Systems — Brad Frost
insights: Three-tier token architecture for theming. Tier 1: raw materials (abstract). Tier 2: semantic mappings. Tier 3: component-specific. Supports white-labeling, dark mode, multiple brands without component changes

title: Design Tokens Course — Brad Frost and Ian Frost
insights: Enterprise-proven three-tier token architecture in Figma + code. Brad and Ian Frost. Recommends Web Components for interoperable themeable core systems

title: Creating Themeable Design Systems — Brad Frost
insights: Token tier architecture secret sauce for multi-brand support. In April 2024 webinar: three-tier + legacy migration + light/dark modes. Practical implementation patterns
